cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this dilemma. So, the person is a single parent who has planned a camping trip with their kids for the weekend. Then the employer calls and asks them to work because there's an urgent project. They need to decide whether to prioritize their job to secure financial stability or keep their promise to the kids for the camping trip. First, I need to consider all the perspectives and ethical principles involved here. Let me start by breaking down the key elements. On the employer's side, the urgent project probably means there's some business need that requires immediate attention. Maybe there's a deadline, a client expecting something, or a major loss if the project isn't completed. The employer might be relying on the employee to fulfill their role, which is part of the job commitment. From a deontological perspective, which focuses on duties and obligations, the employer might argue that the employee has a duty to fulfill their job responsibilities as per the contract. So, working could be seen as the right thing to do based on one's professional obligations. On the other hand, the parent has made a promise to their children, who are relying on
